Composite tilt: LOW — internal cleanliness dominates a moderate macro headwind, and the disciplined POWERGRID exit at the open is a positive behavioral signal.

findings: DISCIPLINED OPENING-BAR EXIT: POWERGRID sold at -₹159 (-1.05%/share, 0.016% of capital) at 09:09:47, within 10 minutes of market open. The lone Power-sector name was cut before any oil-shock-related drawdown could compound on a 4.83%-of-capital position. This matches the Day-7 behavioral pattern of small-loss tactical exits — strategy is reading the risk-off macro tape correctly. No re-entry pattern yet.

SECTOR PROFILE FURTHER CLEANED: 6 sectors → 5 (Power gone). Largest BFSI 12.93% retains 15.07 pp buffer to the loosened 0.28 cap; largest single name NESTLEIND 12.07% retains 2.93 pp buffer to 0.15 cap. The 09:00 max_sector_pct loosening (0.22 → 0.28) remains correctly calibrated — no breach risk emerged from the GLENMARK fill (Pharma 10.05% sits 3.95 pp under the revocation trigger).

DEPLOYMENT IS NOW EVEN LIGHTER: 44.79% deployed (4.82 pp lower than 09:00 full-fill projection of 49.61%) after POWERGRID exit and GLENMARK fill net out. Cash 55.21% (₹549,550). The 0.65 cap is non-binding by 20.21 pp. This is the lightest carrying cash position of the experiment — under-deployment cost is real, but it remains SIGNAL-GATED (strategy not finding entries on risk-off open) not PARAMETER-GATED. Loosening max_deployed_pct to 0.70 would not directly increase deployment.
ORDER BOOK FULLY CLEAR: open_orders count 1 → 0 after GLENMARK fill. First time today the order book is empty. Strategy now has full discretion on next entry timing without any in-flight commitments.

DAILY P&L IS TRIVIAL: -₹159 (-0.016% of capital) — 469x buffer to base 0.075 daily-loss circuit breaker, 125x buffer to override 0.05 (if armed). Loss-gate tightening is not actionable with current realized P&L.

MIN_CONFIDENCE FLOOR STILL NOT EATING TOP-TIER SIGNAL: zero admissions today so no live observations; yesterday's 4 blocks at 0.60-0.62 confirm none of the gated attempts cleared 0.70. By the prompt's explicit criterion ('high-confidence (≥0.70) entries being blocked'), the floor remains correctly calibrated. Hold per Day-10 milestone plan.

GLENMARK CARRY-OVER FILL DOES NOT ESCALATE THE SL-BAND BREACH PRECEDENT: GLENMARK was admitted Apr-29 with 4.4% SL (warning-not-block on engine permissiveness). Today's fill is a passive broker match on a Day-7-submitted order — not a fresh admission, so it does not represent a new instance of the breach pattern. The system_prompt rule 5 hardening (added Apr-29 EOD) governs future admissions; today's fill is grandfathered. SL-band overrides remain correctly calibrated (default 0.015, min 0.01, max 0.04).

MACRO BACKDROP UNCHANGED FROM 09:00: Brent ~$120, FII -₹2,468 Cr (Apr 29), USD/INR 94.85, Big Tech overnight prints driving sentiment, ~47 NSE companies reporting today (HINDUNILVR FMCG bellwether, ADANIPORTS Infra, ADANIENT, BAJAJFINSV, ADANIPOWER all on tape). Sector-theme entries are LIKELY through the day if signals materialize — the loosened 0.28 max_sector_pct gives strategy room to express a theme on conviction without forcing the prior cap to bite. Loosening direction is appropriate; tightening volume-of-admission (max_deployed_pct) into the risk-off open would still be wrong asymmetry.
actions_considered_but_not_taken: FURTHER LOOSEN max_sector_pct (0.28 → 0.32 or full revert to base 0.35): NOT taken. The 09:00 loosening from 0.22 to 0.28 is only 30 minutes old; cap is now 15.07 pp non-binding. A second loosening on the next tick would (a) violate the 'one precise loosening per tick' discipline established in prior assessments, (b) over-rotate against the strategy agent's stated Day-10 holistic review milestone, and (c) eliminate the 7 pp of below-base discipline the 0.28 step deliberately preserved. Hold pending Day-10 review or new evidence.

LOOSEN max_deployed_pct (0.65 → 0.70): NOT taken. Cap is non-binding by 20.21 pp (deepened from 15.39 pp at 09:00 by POWERGRID exit). Under-deployment is signal-gated — strategy is correctly restrained on a risk-off open with Brent +8% and Big Tech overnight reaction pending. Loosening volume-of-admission into this regime has worse asymmetry than holding. Pre-armed for 11:00 re-evaluation if (a) ≥2 high-conf entries admitted by then and (b) full-fill deployment > 60%.

LOOSEN min_confidence (0.65 → 0.60): NOT taken. By the prompt's criterion 'high-confidence (≥0.70) entries being blocked', the floor is NOT eating top-tier signal — yesterday's 4 blocks were all 0.60-0.62; none ≥0.70. Closed-trade n=10 lacks statistical gradient (winners 0.65-0.75, losers 0.65-0.72). Hold per Day-10 milestone plan.

LOOSEN max_position_pct (0.15 → 0.18): NOT taken. NESTLEIND 12.07% largest holds 2.93 pp buffer; cap is not binding. Loosening with no firing trigger is theatrical and would slightly weaken the AUBANK/MAHABANK-blueprint defense.

LOOSEN SL-band overrides (default 0.015, min 0.01, max 0.04): NOT taken. The Apr-29 GLENMARK 4.4% admission remains a fresh blueprint of the breach pattern these caps defend against. Loosening today would compound the precedent. Strategy review (Apr-29 EOD) recommended PROMOTING max_sl_pct to a hard BLOCK at engine level — that is the right direction.

TIGHTEN any override on the POWERGRID exit: NOT taken. POWERGRID -₹159 is a disciplined small-loss cut, not a distress signal. -0.016% of capital, -1.05% per share. Tightening on this datapoint would punish exactly the behavior the system_prompt is designed to encourage. Behavioral health is positive.

TIGHTEN daily_loss_limit_pct (base 0.075 → override 0.05): NOT taken. Pre-armed trigger required intraday loss > -₹20K within 60 min of open; current -₹159 is 0.8% of that threshold. 469x buffer to base circuit breaker. Loss-gate tightening on a near-flat book is theatrical.

TIGHTEN max_sl_pct (0.04 → 0.035): NOT taken. Pre-armed trigger required a fresh ≥4% SL admission within Day-8 first hour; zero fresh admissions so far. GLENMARK fill is carry-over, excluded by spec. Trigger remains armed until 10:00 then disarms.

ELEVATE risk_level to MEDIUM: NOT taken. Internal portfolio risk is unambiguously LOW; external macro risk is bracketed by existing perimeter.

Composite distress meta-trigger D (HIGH risk_level): re-arms if (i) book P&L < -0.5% intraday AND (ii) any single-name in book < -3% AND (iii) strategy silent ≥ 60 min after market open. All three legs currently dormant — strategy is active and book is essentially flat.
rationale: Day-8 09:30 intraday tick. The 09:30 portfolio is a strict improvement over the 09:00 projection: POWERGRID was exited disciplinedly at the open (-₹159, -1.05%/share, 0.016% of capital), removing the lone Power-sector name; GLENMARK carry-over filled as projected (no escalation of the SL-band breach precedent because it's not a fresh admission). All overrides hold their 09:00 calibration: the loosened max_sector_pct=0.28 remains correctly aligned (BFSI 12.93% retains 15.07 pp buffer; Pharma 10.05% sits 3.95 pp below the revocation trigger); max_deployed_pct=0.65 is non-binding by 20.21 pp (under-deployment is signal-gated, not parameter-gated); min_confidence=0.65 is not eating top-tier signal; SL-band overrides are defending against the active GLENMARK precedent; max_position_pct=0.15 is non-binding. No trigger fired this tick (revocations dormant, tighten triggers dormant). The disciplined POWERGRID exit is positive behavioral evidence — strategy is correctly reading the risk-off macro tape and trimming early rather than holding into a deepening drawdown — which validates the existing perimeter rather than calling for adjustment. One precise loosening at 09:00, one disciplined exit at 09:09, zero adjustments at 09:30 — the cleanest possible posture into a heavy earnings day. Risk level remains LOW; HIGH composite meta-trigger fires 0/3 legs.
